old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-a65","title":"Set up SQLite data store scaffolding","description":"Create the foundational database layer before any service-level persistence. Includes: SQLite connection/pool setup, migration runner (run on startup), shared DB instance wired through dependency injection in server.go. This unblocks both task persistence and stocks watchlist persistence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T13:02:53Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T13:02:53Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-cuc","title":"Add users table migration for auth","description":"Add a users table to the SQLite DB scaffolding. Columns: id (PK), google_id (unique), email, name, avatar_url, created_at. This is the user identity record created/upserted on every successful Google OAuth login. Must run as a startup migration via the migration runner established in the SQLite scaffolding.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:04Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:04Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-mcw","title":"Persist tasks to disk (SQLite or JSON file)","description":"Tasks are stored in-memory only and lost on every server restart. Implement file-based or SQLite persistence so tasks survive restarts. SQLite preferred — single file, no external deps, supports CRUD well. Add migration on startup.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T03:23:06Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:23:06Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-xbv","title":"Persist stocks watchlist to disk","description":"Stocks watchlist (symbols added/removed via API) is in-memory only and resets on restart. Persist to a JSON file or the same SQLite DB as tasks. Should load on startup and save on every mutation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-13T03:23:36Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-13T03:23:36Z"}

new_value: Stocks watchlist migration (005_create_stocks_watchlist) merged in PR #12

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-u7d","title":"Implement Google OAuth 2.0 backend","description":"Implement direct Google OAuth 2.0 / OIDC flow in Go using golang.org/x/oauth2. Endpoints: GET /api/auth/login (redirect to Google), GET /api/auth/callback (exchange code, upsert user record, set signed HTTP-only session cookie), POST /api/auth/logout (clear cookie). Any Google account can sign up (open). Store user in SQLite users table on first login.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:05Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:05Z"}

new_value: Google OAuth backend merged in PR #13

old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-drf","title":"Add auth middleware to protect all API routes","description":"Add Go middleware that validates the signed session cookie on every /api/* request (excluding /api/auth/*). Returns HTTP 401 if missing or invalid. Wire into server.go route setup after the existing Recoverer/CORS/Logging middleware chain.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:41Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:41Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-4k7","title":"Frontend auth layer (login page, AuthContext, protected routes)","description":"Add frontend authentication layer: (1) Login page component with 'Sign in with Google' button that calls GET /api/auth/login. (2) AuthContext/Provider that fetches current user from a GET /api/auth/me endpoint and exposes user state app-wide. (3) Protected route wrapper that redirects unauthenticated users to the login page. (4) Display logged-in user's Google name and avatar in the dashboard header. (5) Logout button that calls POST /api/auth/logout.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:41Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T14:55:41Z"}

new_value: {"description":"Add a profile panel/dropdown accessible from the dashboard header showing: (1) User's Google avatar and display name pulled from the session. (2) Sign out button that calls POST /api/auth/logout and redirects to /login. This supersedes the temporary sign-out button added for testing. API keys are managed by the company and are not user-configurable.","title":"User profile panel (avatar, display name, sign out)"}
old_value: {"id":"daily_dashboard-xbq","title":"User profile panel (avatar, display name, sign out, API keys, preferences)","description":"Add a profile panel/dropdown accessible from the dashboard header showing: (1) User's Google avatar and display name from session. (2) Sign out button. (3) API key management (GNEWS, Finnhub, API Ninjas) stored per-user in DB instead of env vars. (4) User preferences (timezone, location, theme). This supersedes the temporary sign-out button added for testing. Depends on per-user settings (d1h) for the API key/preference storage layer.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:03:32Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-15T01:03:32Z"}

(2) Sign out button that calls POST /api/auth/logout and redirects to /login. This supersedes the temporary sign-out button added for testing. API keys are managed by the company and are not user-configurable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:03:32Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-15T01:07:25Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-nfc","title":"Add React Router for SPA navigation (replace window.location.href)","description":"Currently all auth redirects use window.location.href which causes full page reloads, destroying React state and TanStack Query cache. Adopt react-router-dom with useNavigate() and \u003cNavigate /\u003e so auth transitions are client-side. Affects: App.tsx, ProtectedRoute.tsx, LoginPage.tsx.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:53:09Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:53:09Z"}

{"id":"daily_dashboard-4my","title":"Refactor backend route registration: each handler registers its own routes","description":"Currently all routes are registered in server.go setupRoutes(). Refactor so each handler exposes an AddRoutes(r chi.Router) method that registers its own routes. server.go should only wire up middleware and call each handler's AddRoutes. This makes adding new handlers self-contained and keeps server.go clean.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"57775817+meowmix1337@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:56:19Z","created_by":"Dave Van","updated_at":"2026-03-14T21:56:19Z"}

new_value: Implemented: each handler has AddRoutes(r chi.Router); server.go only wires middleware. PR #18.
